title: ContextFragmentAndBundle
description: Schema for the context-fragment system and the assembled bundle ResponseItems sent in the codex Responses API request.

ContextFragment.description: One unit of context content. Either copies an upstream codex-cli fragment exactly, or is an OpenCode-only fragment with explicit ROLE + markers + body justified in design.md.

id.description: Stable identifier used for dedup / replacement across turns. e.g. 'environment_context', 'agents_md_global', 'agents_md_project', 'opencode_protocol', 'role_identity', 'skill:plan-builder'.

role.description: Wire role in input[] item. Mirrors upstream ROLE constant.

startMarker.description: Opening marker tag (e.g. '<environment_context>', '<opencode_protocol>'). Empty string means body is rendered without wrapping.

endMarker.description: Closing marker. Empty string when startMarker is empty.

body.description: The fragment content (already serialized; no further interpolation). Producer is responsible for byte-stable output across turns when inputs are byte-stable.

source.description: Whether this fragment shape comes from upstream codex-cli or is an OpenCode addition. opencode-only fragments must have a design.md justification.

designAnchor.description: For opencode-only fragments: anchor to the design.md decision or section explaining why.

BundledResponseItem.description: One assembled ResponseItem for the input[] array. Produced by BundleAssembler from a list of ContextFragments sharing the same role.

fragmentIds.description: Ordered list of fragment ids contributing to this bundle, for telemetry / debugging.

RequestEnvelope.description: The final body sent to the Responses API after Stage A.

instructions.description: Driver persona text only. Equals upstream default.md content (or model-specific *_prompt.md when routed). NEVER includes SYSTEM.md, AGENTS.md, agent prompt, identity.

input.description: input[0] is developer-role bundle, input[1] is user-role bundle, then conversation history.

prompt_cache_key.description: Pure threadId / sessionId. Matches upstream `Some(self.state.thread_id.to_string())`. Specifically NOT prefixed with accountId.

previous_response_id.description: Optional, omitted on first call or after chain reset.
